**ACT therapy**: RFT is foundational to Acceptance and Commitment Therapy
**Autism interventions**: PEAK Relational Training System
**Understanding metaphor and analogy**: How frames combine to create complex meaning
**Psychopathology**: Rigidity in relational responding linked to mental health issues
Hayes, Barnes-Holmes, Roche (2001): *Relational Frame Theory: A Post-Skinnerian Account of Human Language and Cognition*
Wikipedia: Comprehensive overview with examples
~300 empirical studies validate core RFT predictions

**Program analysis** (taint tracking, 1970s–present): Classical taint analysis marks data from untrusted/sensitive sources, tracks propagation through program execution, and alerts when tainted data reaches sinks. First application to AI agent sessions: a session is the execution unit, the session log is the trace, and the shared filesystem is the inter-session propagation medium. Key insight: taintedness is transitive and persistent.

**IAEA nuclear material accountancy** (1957–present): The IAEA tracks fissile material across "material balance areas" (MBAs) — defined zones where material is quantified at entry and exit. Any discrepancy between book inventory and physical inventory triggers investigation. Sessions are our MBAs. Sensitive data is nuclear material. Outbound channels are exits. The material balance report accounts for every sensitive data item's full journey.

**Epidemiological contact tracing** (COVID-era graph algorithms): Graph-based tracking of "infection" (sensitive data exposure) through a population (sessions), identifying transmission chains and super-spreader nodes (files touched by many sessions). AGENTS.md identified as a super-spreader file.

**Financial crime — smurfing/structuring** (anti-money-laundering): Breaking a single detectable transaction into multiple sub-threshold transactions across different accounts/branches. The cross-session attack is structurally identical: split the exfiltration across session boundaries so each session's individual monitoring sees nothing suspicious.
`scripts/immune/cross-session-taint.sh` — new antibody with five detection phases:
Phase 1 (Taint Sources): Scans 48h of session logs, identifies sensitive reads (config, credentials, canaries, env vars, SSH keys). Found 141 events across 21 sessions in 2 agents.
Phase 2 (Taint Propagation): Tracks file writes after sensitive reads, marks written files as "tainted." Identified 87 tainted files.

Phase 3 (Cross-Session Sinks): Finds sessions that read tainted files AND make outbound calls. Severity-tiered: P0 (non-main exfil via taint), P1 (main reads non-main taint + outbound), P2 (other cross-session flows). Found 8 P2 findings (all legitimate main→main).
Phase 4 (Contact Graph): Builds agent-to-agent contact edges via shared files. Identifies super-spreader files (5+ sessions). Found main→watchtower edge and AGENTS.md as super-spreader.
Phase 5 (Material Balance): IAEA-style accounting — for each sensitive item, where was it read, where might it have propagated, what outbound channels exist? Missing material = investigation trigger.

`scripts/immune/cross-session-taint-analysis.md` — 115-line theoretical framework documenting the blind spot, all four cross-discipline sources, the five-phase model, limitations (false positives from legitimate work, taint explosion, encoding evasion), and future evolution (xattr-based taint labels, semantic taint, R₀ for data contagion).
Integrated into `run-all.sh` immune system sweep.
Taint decay model (radioactive half-life for data sensitivity — reduces false positives from old taint)
xattr-based file labels (macOS extended attributes to mark tainted files at the filesystem level)
Semantic taint (tracking meaning propagation, not just byte copying)
R₀ calculation for files (reproduction number — which files spread sensitive data most?)
Integration with intent ledger (cross-session intent verification)
Real-time taint enforcement (block tainted file reads in non-main agents)

**Accounting** (double-entry bookkeeping, 1299 AD): Every transaction needs balanced debits and credits. Applied to agents: every sensitive action (credit) needs a corresponding user intent (debit). Imbalanced books = fraud indicator. This is the oldest fraud detection system in human history, and nobody has applied it to AI agent security.

**Economics** (mechanism design / revelation principle, 2007 Nobel): Design the environment so honest behavior is the dominant strategy. By making unjustified actions visible, we change the game theory for compromised agents — they must either manipulate user context (harder) or accept detection.
**Science** (pre-registration, 2013+): Declare hypotheses before experiments to prevent p-hacking. Same principle: user intent must precede agent action. Post-hoc justification is inherently suspect.

**Formal methods** (confused deputy problem, 1988): Agents with ambient authority can be tricked into using permissions beyond what the user intended. The ledger detects when agent actions exceed user intent.
`scripts/immune/intent-ledger.sh` — new antibody that implements a "double-entry security ledger":
Extracts user messages (intents/debits) and tool calls (actions/credits) from session logs
For each sensitive action, checks contextual justification via keyword overlap with preceding user messages
Identifies "orphan actions" — sensitive operations with no user justification
Agent-aware severity: main (trusted) = INFO, non-main = WARNING/P1
Automated session detection (cron/heartbeat = auto-justified)
Generates balance sheet: justified vs unjustified ratio, health rating (≥90% healthy, 70-89% investigate, <70% critical)
Real results: 36 sessions scanned, 634 sensitive actions, 91% balanced, 7 real findings in watchtower agent

`scripts/immune/double-entry-analysis.md` — theoretical framework documenting cross-pollination, the paradigm shift from "is this action dangerous?" to "was this action requested?", and future evolution path (ML-based intent matching, pre-registration protocol, real-time enforcement)
Integrated into `run-all.sh` immune system sweep
ML-based semantic intent matching (replace keyword overlap with embeddings)
Pre-registration protocol: agents declare intent BEFORE acting, creating unforgeable pre-commitment
Cross-session ledger (user asked in session A, agent acts in session B — currently invisible)
Real-time enforcement (block unjustified actions instead of post-hoc detection)
Tune watchtower false positives (open-ended instructions like "fix things" should partially justify broad actions)
Information theory (Shannon entropy for detecting encoded data in tool parameters)
SIGINT/TEMPEST (NSA electromagnetic emanation detection → tool call "emanation" monitoring)
Ecology (cuckoo brood parasitism — encoding deceptive information through legitimate channels; hosts evolved pattern recognition, so must our monitors)
Nuclear security (two-person rule for high-risk operations)
`scripts/immune/covert-channel-analyzer.sh` — new antibody that detects:
A) URL/query entropy anomalies (Shannon entropy + encoding pattern detection)
B) Delayed exfiltration chains (sensitive read → outbound call, gap-agnostic)
C) Cross-channel exfiltration (file read → message send to Discord/WhatsApp/Signal)
D) Chunked exfiltration (repeated high-entropy requests to same domain)
E) Covert channel bandwidth report (quantifies theoretical capacity per tool)
`scripts/immune/covert-channel-analysis.md` — first-ever enumeration of covert channels in an AI agent tool interface, with bandwidth calculations and detection strategies
Integrated into run-all.sh immune system sweep
Agent-aware severity: main agent (trusted) uses higher thresholds; non-main agents trigger 🚨 P0 alerts for any suspicious pattern
Cross-session information flow tracking (read in session A, exfil in session B via shared filesystem)
Timing channel detection (inter-call timing analysis)
Semantic steganography detection in natural language messages (the hardest problem)
URL canonicalization to reduce encoding capacity
